own_review_points: getCheckCondition(String,String[]) (line 330) still appends values without escaping single quotes (no value.replace) -> correctness/injection issue, same as PR 11844

timestampdiffPattern wraps everything in cast(... as bigint); fractional results (e.g. /86400, *1e9) get truncated toward zero, and MONTH/QUARTER use only year+month extract, ignoring day-of-month -> month-diff semantics differ from a true date diff
SPANNER_PG enum inserted right before DERBY, out of alphabetical order
SpannerPostgreSQLTruncFunction passes false to parent then routes numeric vs datetime trunc by arg type
getLimitHandler returns LimitOffsetLimitHandler (no FETCH FIRST) consistent with supportsFetchClause=false
notes: 193-file PR, diff truncated at 150k (141k more unseen). Human reference is empty ('PR is huge'), so missed counts only from own review. All v8 points are grounded in the visible diff and none are factually wrong. Six are substantive (FIXIT placeholder, extract(epoch) assumption, missing @Override, fragile sequence-table heuristic, trunc routing, limit handler). Three are pure style/verify nits (alphabetical enum ordering, generic skip-reason wording, CI port range) -> trivial. v8's 'no test exercises timestampdiff' sub-claim is unverifiable given truncation but the core assumption-flag stands. Missed the unescaped getCheckCondition(String[]) bug and the bigint-cast/month-diff timestampdiff semantics. Broad but nit-heavy review.
